text.title: AI-Enabled Business Analysis (Business Analyst) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI-Enabled Business Analysis (Business Analyst) application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Methodology scopes to the business-analyst role; cross-role concerns linked to the ba-core flavour.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis (Business Analyst) authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Mixed-role methodologies become unmaintainable; role-scope makes ownership clear.

rule.statement: BA function publishes a written governance policy for LLM use; reviewed quarterly.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis (Business Analyst) authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Verbal governance drifts within weeks; written + cadence keeps it current.

rule.statement: Each requirement names a stakeholder source.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis (Business Analyst) authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.statement: AI initiative classified per AI TRiSM before BA sign-off.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis (Business Analyst) authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.statement: For agentic systems, BA spec names boundaries, escalation rules, and human-oversight requirements.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis (Business Analyst) authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
